items.label: ADR 0002 Contract Versioning by Block Range

architecture/argocd.title: ArgoCD — GitOps Continuous Delivery
architecture/argocd.description: Pull-based GitOps continuous delivery using ArgoCD for LuckyPlans.

architecture/ci-cd-pipeline.title: CI/CD Pipeline
architecture/ci-cd-pipeline.description: Automated pipeline using GitHub Actions and ArgoCD for the LuckyPlans monorepo.

architecture/copy-trading-engine.title: Copy-Trading Engine
architecture/copy-trading-engine.description: LuckyPlans combines historical research with live copy-trading workflows. The live engine and the simulation engine share concepts, but they should not be treated as the same operational mode.

architecture/data-flow.title: Data Flow
architecture/data-flow.description: The product flow is easiest to understand as a pipeline from raw venue data into research and execution surfaces.

architecture/database-design.title: Database Design
architecture/database-design.description: LuckyPlans uses PostgreSQL through Prisma. The current implementation is broad, but the table below captures the main concepts without dumping the full schema.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-10-functi.title: Functional Decomposition over Domain-Driven Services
architecture/decisions/2026-03-10-functi.description: Chose functional decomposition (service-core for all CRUD) over domain-per-service DDD.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-10-graphq.title: GraphQL Code-First via API Gateway
architecture/decisions/2026-03-10-graphq.description: Chose GraphQL code-first with NestJS decorators over REST or schema-first GraphQL.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-10-redis-.title: Redis Pub/Sub for Inter-Service Communication
architecture/decisions/2026-03-10-redis-.description: Chose Redis pub/sub via NestJS Transport.REDIS over HTTP, gRPC, or full message brokers.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-11-graphq.title: Switch GraphQL Codegen to client-preset with Inline Operations
architecture/decisions/2026-03-11-graphq.description: Replaced three separate codegen plugins and .graphql files with client-preset and inline graphql() calls.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-11-keyclo.title: Keycloak OIDC + next-auth v5 for Authentication
architecture/decisions/2026-03-11-keyclo.description: Adopt Keycloak as the IdP with Authorization Code Flow + PKCE via next-auth v5; validate tokens in the API Gateway using JWKS.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-11-nextra.title: Nextra v4 Integrated at /docs Route
architecture/decisions/2026-03-11-nextra.description: Integrated Nextra v4 (without nextra-theme-docs) at the /docs route inside apps/web.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-12-custom.title: Custom Login/Register Pages with ROPC + Keycloak Admin API
architecture/decisions/2026-03-12-custom.description: Replace Keycloak UI redirects and nginx proxy with custom Next.js auth pages, gateway ROPC login, Admin API registration, and Next.js rewrites.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-12-gatewa.title: Gateway-Managed Sessions (replacing next-auth)
architecture/decisions/2026-03-12-gatewa.description: Move OAuth token management from the Next.js frontend into the API Gateway with Redis-backed server-side sessions.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-13-bitnam.title: Bitnami Sealed Secrets for Production
architecture/decisions/2026-03-13-bitnam.description: Use Bitnami Sealed Secrets to manage production secrets safely in Git.

architecture/decisions/2026-03-16-prisma.title: PostgreSQL Persistence with Prisma ORM
architecture/decisions/2026-03-16-prisma.description: Add PostgreSQL as the application database via Prisma ORM in a dedicated packages/prisma package

architecture/decisions/2026-05-21-gatewa.title: Gateway-Owned Domain Logic with External Edge Orchestration
architecture/decisions/2026-05-21-gatewa.description: Current architecture keeps client-facing domain logic in api-gateway and uses external edge runtimes for distributed execution.

architecture/decisions/2026-06-05-defer-.title: Defer Edge WebSocket Sessions
architecture/decisions/2026-06-05-defer-.description: Current edge orchestration remains on authenticated internal REST polling and connectivity heartbeats; persistent WebSocket sessions are deferred until low-latency command needs justify them.

architecture/decisions/2026-06-05-defer-.title: Defer Generic Task Artifact Transfer
architecture/decisions/2026-06-05-defer-.description: Current edge task payloads and results remain on internal REST JSON endpoints; object-storage artifact transfer is deferred until task data grows beyond that contract.

architecture/decisions/2026-06-25-standa.title: Standalone Docs App on docs.luckyplans.xyz
architecture/decisions/2026-06-25-standa.description: Split the public docs and blog out of apps/web into a dedicated Docusaurus deployment.

architecture/helm-deployment.title: Helm Deployment Architecture
architecture/helm-deployment.description: Single Helm chart design, service configuration, and deployment commands.

architecture/indexing-pipeline.title: Indexing Pipeline
architecture/indexing-pipeline.description: The indexing pipeline is what turns raw platform logs into researchable history.

architecture/infrastructure.title: Infrastructure
architecture/infrastructure.description: LuckyPlans currently has two infrastructure stories that matter:

architecture/overview.title: System Overview
architecture/overview.description: LuckyPlans currently spans three practical layers:

architecture/security-model.title: Security Model
architecture/security-model.description: LuckyPlans mixes public docs, private product workflows, on-chain analysis, and execution-adjacent behavior. That means security has to be explained in terms of boundaries, not slogans.

architecture/service-map.title: Service Map
architecture/service-map.description: This map reflects the real implementation in the current frontend and backend repositories, plus the current deployment assets in the beta monorepo.

architecture/simulation-engine.title: Simulation Engine
architecture/simulation-engine.description: The current LuckyPlans backend treats simulation as a first-class product workflow.

architecture/tls-certificates.title: TLS Certificates — cert-manager + Let's Encrypt
architecture/tls-certificates.description: Automatic TLS provisioning and renewal using cert-manager with Let's Encrypt ACME HTTP-01.

contributing/architecture-decisions.title: Architecture Decisions
contributing/architecture-decisions.description: LuckyPlans uses ADRs to document technical choices that shape product behavior, historical correctness, or operational cost.

guides/add-new-chain.title: Add New Chain
guides/add-new-chain.description: Adding a chain is usually smaller than adding a new platform, but it still affects contract metadata, RPC routing, and historical interpretation.

guides/add-new-contract-version.title: Add New Contract Version
guides/add-new-contract-version.description: The current backend uses contract metadata to decide which parser and range should be applied to historical logs.

guides/add-new-platform.title: Add New Platform
guides/add-new-platform.description: Use this workflow when adding support for a new perp venue to the current backend.

guides/analyze-leaderboard.description: The current frontend exposes leaderboard flows at:

guides/create-simulation.description: The current frontend includes real simulation routes for:

guides/deployment.title: Deployment Guide
guides/deployment.description: Deploy LuckyPlans to local k3d or production Kubernetes with ArgoCD GitOps.

guides/deployment-guide.title: Deployment Guide
guides/deployment-guide.description: This guide documents the deploy path that is actually present in the beta monorepo today.

guides/developer.title: Developer Guide
guides/developer.description: How to set up and run LuckyPlans locally for development.

guides/quickstart.title: Quickstart
guides/quickstart.description: This quickstart is based on the current working product implementation in the split repositories:

index.title: LuckyPlans Documentation
index.description: Product, architecture, and operations documentation for the current LuckyPlans implementation.

intro/what-is-luckyplans.title: What is LuckyPlans?
intro/what-is-luckyplans.description: LuckyPlans is a research and operations platform for perpetual DEX trading workflows.

reference/api-reference.title: API Reference
reference/api-reference.description: The current product API is GraphQL-first and exposed by the backend API_SERVICE.

reference/configuration-reference.title: Configuration Reference
reference/configuration-reference.description: LuckyPlans configuration currently spans two implementation families:

reference/error-codes.title: Error Codes
reference/error-codes.description: LuckyPlans does not currently publish a formal cross-service error-code catalog in the product repos.

reference/graphql-reference.title: GraphQL Reference
reference/graphql-reference.description: This page summarizes the current GraphQL surface from the real backend and frontend schema files.

reference/helm-values.title: Helm Values
reference/helm-values.description: The beta monorepo Helm chart lives at infrastructure/helm/luckyplans.

reference/message-patterns.title: Message Patterns
reference/message-patterns.description: The current backend uses NestJS Redis microservice transport with explicit message and event pattern names.

roadmap/roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap/roadmap.description: The roadmap below is stage-based on purpose. It reflects direction without pretending exact dates are already locked.
